objective:
Create internal/provider/parse.go (sibling file in the EXISTING `package provider` from P1.M2.T1.S1; plain `package provider` line — manifest.go owns the package doc) implementing the unexported `func parseOutput(raw string, m Manifest) (string, bool)` — the PRD §12.9 robust output-parsing pipeline: (1) `s = strings.TrimSpace(raw)`; (2) if `m.StripCodeFence && (strings.HasPrefix(s, "```")||strings.HasPrefix(s, "~~~"))` then `fence := s[:3]`, drop the opener line (everything through the first `
`, or the whole string if no `
`), cut from the LAST `strings.LastIndex(body, fence)` onward, re-trim; (3) output switch where `m.Output == ""` (default) and `"raw"` → `msg = s`, and ONLY `m.Output == "json"` → `extractJSON(s, m.JSONField)` with ANY failure silently falling back to raw `msg=s` (§17.4 robustness); (4) normalize `strings.ReplaceAll(msg, "
", "
")` THEN collapse 3+ `
`→`

` via a package-level `regexp.MustCompile("
{3,}")`; (5) `msg = strings.TrimSpace(msg); return msg, msg != ""`. Add an unexported helper `func extractJSON(s, field string) (string, bool)` that tries `json.Unmarshal([]byte(s), &map[string]any)` then, on failure, the brace-balanced substring `s[firstIndexByte('{') : lastIndexByte('}')+1]` (guard end>start), extracting `obj[field].(string)` with STRICT string type assertion (non-string/missing/empty field ⇒ `("", false)` ⇒ raw fallback). parseOutput reads ONLY `m.Output`/`m.JSONField`/`m.StripCodeFence` and returns `(msg, ok)` — NEVER an error; do NOT import `fmt`/`log`/`os`/config (keep it pure; verbose-logging of the fallback event is the generate layer P1.M6.T1.S1's concern, not this function's). Deliver WHITE-BOX table-driven tests (internal/provider/parse_test.go, `package provider`, stdlib `testing` only) covering: raw clean, fenced-raw, fenced-raw-with-language-tag, tilde fence, fenced-json, json-embedded-in-prose, malformed-json→fallback-to-raw, json-non-string-field→fallback, newline normalization (
 + 3+ collapse), fence-strip-off keeps the fence, empty→("",false), whitespace-only→("",false). DOCS impact: Mode A — godoc on parseOutput + extractJSON citing PRD §12.9 and §17.4.
implementationSteps:
Create `internal/provider/parse.go` as a SIBLING file in the EXISTING `package provider` (the package + manifest.go already exist from P1.M2.T1.S1). Use a PLAIN `package provider` line — do NOT repeat the `// Package provider` doc comment (manifest.go owns it; a duplicate package comment is a vet/lint error). Import ONLY stdlib: `encoding/json`, `regexp`, `strings` — NO `os`, `log`, `fmt`, config, cobra, or go-toml. Define a package-level `var collapseNewlines = regexp.MustCompile("
{3,}")` (compile once). Implement the UNEXPORTED `func parseOutput(raw string, m Manifest) (string, bool)` per PRD §12.9 byte-exactly: (1) `s := strings.TrimSpace(raw)`; (2) fence unwrap — enter ONLY when `m.StripCodeFence && (strings.HasPrefix(s, "```") || strings.HasPrefix(s, "~~~"))`; set `fence := s[:3]` (``` matches only ```, ~~~ matches only ~~~); drop the opener line via `if nl := strings.IndexByte(s, '
'); nl != -1 { s = s[nl+1:] } else { s = "" }` (drops opener incl. any language tag); cut from the LAST closer via `if last := strings.LastIndex(s, fence); last != -1 { s = s[:last] }` (search the BODY after opener removal, NOT the original s); `s = strings.TrimSpace(s)`; (3) output switch — `msg := s`; `if m.Output == OutputJSON { if v, ok := extractJSON(s, m.JSONField); ok { msg = v } }` — i.e. the DEFAULT `""` and `"raw"` both yield msg=s, and ONLY `"json"` attempts extraction, with ANY failure silently leaving msg=s (raw fallback, §17.4); (4) normalize — `msg = strings.ReplaceAll(msg, "
", "
")` FIRST, then `msg = collapseNewlines.ReplaceAllString(msg, "

")` (do NOT touch lone ``); (5) `msg = strings.TrimSpace(msg); return msg, msg != ""`. Reuse the existing `OutputJSON` const from manifest.go — do NOT redefine it. parseOutput reads ONLY m.Output/m.JSONField/m.StripCodeFence and returns (msg, ok), NEVER an error. Add godoc on parseOutput citing PRD §12.9 + §17.4 and noting the fallback-to-raw behavior and that verbose logging of a fallback event is the generate layer's (P1.M6.T1.S1) concern.

Implement the UNEXPORTED helper `func extractJSON(s, field string) (string, bool)` in the same parse.go file: `var obj map[string]any`; first attempt `json.Unmarshal([]byte(s), &obj)` — on success, return `v, true` where `v, ok := obj[field].(string)` (STRICT string type assertion; non-string/missing/empty field ⇒ falls through); second attempt (balanced-substring retry) — `start := strings.IndexByte(s, '{')`, `end := strings.LastIndexByte(s, '}')`, guard `start != -1 && end != -1 && end > start`, slice `s[start:end+1]`, retry `json.Unmarshal` into a fresh `map[string]any`, again strict `.(string)` assertion; return `("", false)` on ANY failure (caller parseOutput falls back to raw). Add godoc citing PRD §12.9 step 3 (whole-then-balanced retry) and §17.4 (raw fallback). json.Unmarshal returns an error (never panics) on malformed input, so no recover is needed.

Create `internal/provider/parse_test.go` as `package provider` (WHITE-BOX — parseOutput is unexported, so the test must live in the same package, like manifest_test.go) using stdlib `testing` ONLY (no go.mod change). Write a table-driven `TestParseOutput` (use `tc := tc` + `t.Run(tc.name, ...)`) with these exact cases (input raw, Manifest, expected msg, expected ok): (a) raw clean — `"feat: add parser"`, {Output:"raw",StripCodeFence:true} → "feat: add parser", true; (b) fenced-raw — `"```
feat: add parser
```"`, {Output:"raw",StripCodeFence:true} → "feat: add parser", true; (c) fenced-raw-with-langtag — `"```text
feat: x
```"`, {Output:"raw",StripCodeFence:true} → "feat: x", true; (d) tilde fence — `"~~~
feat: x
~~~"`, {Output:"raw",StripCodeFence:true} → "feat: x", true; (e) fenced-json — `"```json
{"result":"feat: x"}
```"`, {Output:"json",JSONField:"result",StripCodeFence:true} → "feat: x", true; (f) json-embedded-in-prose — `"Sure! {"result":"feat: x"} hope this helps"`, {Output:"json",JSONField:"result",StripCodeFence:true} → "feat: x", true; (g) malformed-json→fallback-to-raw — `"{"result": feat: x}"`, {Output:"json",JSONField:"result",StripCodeFence:true} → "{"result": feat: x}", true; (h) json-non-string-field→fallback — `"{"result": 42}"`, {Output:"json",JSONField:"result",StripCodeFence:false} → "{"result": 42}", true; (i) newline-normalization — `"feat: x


body"`, {Output:"raw",StripCodeFence:true} → "feat: x

body", true; (j) fence-strip-OFF keeps fence — `"```
feat: x
```"`, {Output:"raw",StripCodeFence:false} → "```
feat: x
```", true; (k) empty — `""`, {Output:"raw",StripCodeFence:true} → "", false; (l) whitespace-only — `"   
	  
"`, {Output:"raw",StripCodeFence:true} → "", false. Also add `TestParseOutput_EmptyOutputIsRaw` (m.Output="" with a clean message ⇒ msg=that message, ok=true — proves the default is raw, not json). For each case assert both the returned msg (string equality) and ok (bool) exactly. You MAY reuse `sixBuiltinManifests()` from manifest_test.go to build a realistic pi fixture (Output=raw, StripCodeFence=true) for an extra integration-flavored case, but the core table uses inline Manifest literals for determinism.

Run the validation gates IN ORDER and fix any failure before declaring done: `go build ./internal/provider/` (compiles), `go vet ./internal/provider/` (clean — also catches a duplicate package comment), `test -z "$(gofmt -l internal/provider/)"` (no unformatted files), `go test ./internal/provider/` (PASS — full parse table + the existing manifest tests still pass), `go test ./...` (whole-module integrity; Makefile `test` target green; internal/ui tests still pass).

DOCS impact: Mode A — confirm `parseOutput` and `extractJSON` each have a godoc comment citing PRD §12.9 and §17.4 (raw-default rationale) plus the fallback-to-raw behavior. Create NO README.md/docs/providers/*.toml here. Confirm scope boundaries: only `internal/provider/parse.go` and `internal/provider/parse_test.go` were created; manifest.go, manifest_test.go, main.go, Makefile, go.mod, go.sum, and internal/ui are unchanged; only stdlib imports were added (encoding/json, regexp, strings); no `go mod tidy`; the `// Package provider` doc comment still appears exactly once (in manifest.go) and parse.go uses a plain `package provider` line.

successCriteria.description: `internal/provider/parse.go` exists as a SIBLING file in the EXISTING `package provider` with a PLAIN `package provider` line (the `// Package provider` doc remains solely in manifest.go) and imports ONLY stdlib (`encoding/json`, `regexp`, `strings`); no `os`, `log`, `fmt`, config, cobra, or go-toml import.

`parseOutput(raw string, m Manifest) (string, bool)` implements the 5-step PRD §12.9 pipeline byte-exactly: (1) TrimSpace; (2) optional single-layer fence unwrap entered only when `m.StripCodeFence && (HasPrefix "```" or "~~~")`, using `fence := s[:3]`, dropping the opener line (through first `
`), cutting from the LAST closer in the post-opener body, then re-trim; (3) output switch where `""` (default) and `"raw"` → msg=s and ONLY `"json"` attempts extraction with silent raw fallback on any failure; (4) `
`→`
` then collapse 3+ `
`→`

`; (5) TrimSpace and `ok = msg != ""`. It reads ONLY m.Output/m.JSONField/m.StripCodeFence and returns (msg, ok), never an error.

`extractJSON(s, field string) (string, bool)` (unexported helper) tries `json.Unmarshal` into `map[string]any` on the whole string, then on the brace-balanced substring `s[firstIndexByte('{') : lastIndexByte('}')+1]` (guarded end>start), extracting `obj[field]` with a STRICT `.(string)` type assertion; a non-string/missing/empty field or any Unmarshal failure returns `("", false)` so parseOutput falls back to raw.

`parseOutput` stays PURE: no `log`/`os`/config import and no attempt to emit a "fallback flag" log line — the fallback-to-raw BEHAVIOR (json fails ⇒ msg=s, identical to raw success) is the contract, and verbose logging of the fallback event is explicitly deferred to the generate layer (P1.M6.T1.S1).

`internal/provider/parse_test.go` is `package provider` (white-box, stdlib `testing` only) and its table-driven tests pass for: raw clean, fenced-raw, fenced-raw-with-language-tag, tilde fence, fenced-json, json-embedded-in-prose, malformed-json→fallback-to-raw, json-non-string-field→fallback, newline normalization (`\r\n` + 3+ newline collapse), fence-strip-OFF keeps the fence, empty→("",false), whitespace-only→("",false); plus a case proving empty `m.Output` is treated as raw (not json).

`go test ./internal/provider/` exits 0 (parse table + the existing manifest golden tests still pass) and `go test ./...` exits 0 (whole-module integrity; Makefile `test` target green; internal/ui tests still pass).
`go build ./internal/provider/` and `go vet ./internal/provider/` are clean, and `gofmt -l internal/provider/` produces no output.

Scope boundaries respected: only internal/provider/parse.go and internal/provider/parse_test.go created; manifest.go, manifest_test.go, main.go, Makefile, go.mod, go.sum, and internal/ui unchanged; no README/docs/providers TOML created; no `go mod tidy`; the `// Package provider` doc comment appears exactly once (in manifest.go). Mode A docs satisfied: godoc on parseOutput + extractJSON cites PRD §12.9 and §17.4.

references: PRD.md §12.9 (lines 754-766) — THE output-parsing pipeline spec (5 steps: trim, fence unwrap, raw/json switch+fallback, newline normalize, trim; ok=msg!=""). file:///home/dustin/projects/stagehand-hack/PRD.md

PRD.md §17.4 (lines 1142-1148) — WHY raw output not JSON (decision D1): raw removes the JSON-escaping footgun + the 'no double quotes' constraint; JSON remains an option; fallback-to-raw is the robustness philosophy. file:///home/dustin/projects/stagehand-hack/PRD.md

PRD.md §9.6 FR26-FR28 (lines 273-275) — functional requirements: FR26 raw default; FR27 json via json_field; FR28 robust pipeline (strip ws, unwrap one fence layer, json whole-then-first-block, raw fallback). file:///home/dustin/projects/stagehand-hack/PRD.md

plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/decisions.md §4 — restates the identical 5-step pipeline and names the exact table-driven test matrix (raw, fenced-raw, fenced-json, json-in-prose, fallback-to-raw, empty→ok=false).
plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/reference_impl.md — D1 (table: raw default replaces commit-pi's JSON+sed) + §7 (the robust parse pipeline is genuinely NEW work, not a port).

internal/provider/manifest.go — THE dependency (P1.M2.T1.S1): the Manifest struct with Output/JSONField/StripCodeFence fields (marked 'Consumed by the output parser, not by Render') and the OutputRaw/OutputJSON string constants to reuse; also the stdlib-only purity + godoc density + plain-sibling-file package-line conventions to follow.
internal/provider/manifest_test.go — repo test convention (table-driven white-box: tc:=tc, t.Run, t.Fatalf/t.Errorf) and the sixBuiltinManifests() helper available for realistic Manifest fixtures.
internal/ui/output_test.go — stdlib-testing table-driven convention (tc := tc + t.Run; reflect.DeepEqual for value comparison).

plan/001_f1f80943ac34/prps/research/P1_M2_T2_S1_research.md — THIS task's research: resolves the 3 ambiguities (closer search scope = post-opener body; fallback flag = keep pure, logging deferred to generate; parseOutput stays unexported ⇒ white-box tests) and includes a validated 12-case throwaway prototype (all PASS).

plan/001_f1f80943ac34/prps/P1_M2_T1_S1.json — completed dependency PRP (the Manifest type this task consumes; same gofmt-gate idiom `test -z "$(gofmt -l ...)"`, same Mode-A godoc style, same scope discipline).
encoding/json.Unmarshal — decodes into map[string]any; returns an error (never panics) on malformed input, so no recover is needed. https://pkg.go.dev/encoding/json#Unmarshal
regexp.MustCompile — package-level compiled regexp for the 3+-newline collapse (MustCompile panics only on an invalid pattern; ours `\n{3,}` is valid). https://pkg.go.dev/regexp#MustCompile
strings package — TrimSpace (steps 1+5), IndexByte/LastIndexByte/LastIndex/HasPrefix/ReplaceAll (fence + brace-balanced-substring logic). https://pkg.go.dev/strings
